title: 2026 Oscar Predictions: Will the Best Screenplays Drive the Best Picture Win?
text: According to Sasha Stone's predictions, the Original Screenplay race features five main contenders: Sinners; Marty Supreme; Sentimental Value; Weapons; It Was Just An Accident. With Eddington listed as an alternative, Stone notes that Sinners notably did not win the Golden Globes in this category, which she interprets as potentially problematic: "Sinners didn't win Screenplay there, which only tells us that these voters probably didn't get the movie." Adapted Screenplay Category: The adapted category predictions include: One Battle After Another; Hamnet; Train Dreams; Bugonia; Frankenstein. Stone highlights that Hamnet's screenplay was "adapted by Chloé Zhao and the original novel's author, Maggie O'Farrell," with the adaptation being substantially different from its source material. Screenplay's Role in Best Picture: Stone explores whether screenplays alone can drive Best Picture wins without Best Director victories, noting this pattern has largely disappeared in recent years. She observes that when a film wins multiple major precursor awards (Globes, PGA, DGA, SAG), it typically secures Best Picture in the first round of voting.
